title: Quiz: Reaction Mechanisms on Surfaces
description: Test your understanding of surface reaction mechanisms

questions.question: What is the key feature of a transition state on a potential energy surface?

options.text: It has the lowest energy along the pathway
It is a saddle point with one imaginary frequency
It has all positive vibrational frequencies

questions.explanation: A transition state is a saddle point on the potential energy surface. It has exactly one imaginary frequency, corresponding to motion along the reaction coordinate, with all other frequencies being real (positive).

questions.question: In the Nudged Elastic Band (NEB) method, what is the role of spring forces?

options.text: To accelerate convergence to the minimum
To keep images evenly distributed along the path
To push the path toward lower energies

questions.explanation: Spring forces in NEB prevent images from sliding down toward reactant or product minima, keeping them evenly distributed along the reaction path. This ensures proper sampling of the minimum energy pathway.

questions.question: Which mechanism involves reaction between an adsorbed species and a gas-phase molecule?
questions.explanation: The Eley-Rideal mechanism involves reaction between an adsorbed species and a gas-phase molecule (A* + B(g) → C*). Langmuir-Hinshelwood requires both species to be adsorbed.

questions.question: Why does adsorption typically have a positive -TΔS contribution to free energy?

options.text: Molecules gain rotational freedom on the surface
Molecules lose translational and rotational degrees of freedom

questions.explanation: When molecules adsorb, they lose their translational and rotational degrees of freedom (3 each for non-linear molecules), resulting in a large entropy decrease. Since ΔS < 0, the -TΔS term is positive, making adsorption less favorable at higher temperatures.

questions.question: The rate-determining step is always the step with the highest activation barrier.
questions.explanation: False. The rate-determining step depends on coverage, reaction conditions, and the relative energies of intermediates. The apparent activation energy is measured from the most stable intermediate to the highest TS, not necessarily the largest individual barrier.

questions.question: Which contributions must be included when converting DFT energies to Gibbs free energies? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Gibbs free energy requires: ZPE (quantum vibrational effects), thermal enthalpy (temperature effects), and entropy (-TS term). Exchange-correlation is already included in the DFT electronic energy.
